english: I. sbst. 1. mother, col. ǎ-ma (a ma), resp. yum (yum); mai rum (ma'i rum) womb, matrix; ráṅ-gi ma ɣcíg-pai sríṅ-mo (rang gi ma gcig pa'i sring mo) full sister by the same mother, whilst mas dbén-pai sriṅ-mo (mas dben pa'i sring mo) denotes half-sister, step-sister, by another mother. - 2. frq. used metonymically, e.g. capital, v. below; *ma tsam yod* (*ma tsam yod*) W., what is the amount of the sum advanced? original text, copy to write after, pattern v. below; a letter written above another. - Comp.: ma-kál (ma khal) amount in bushels of grain lent out. - ma-kú (ma khu) mother and uncle, v. kú-bo (khu bo). - ma-rgyúd (ma rgyud) Sch. 1. original, primary cause. 2. line of descent by the mother's side, when however it should be spelt brgyud (brgyud). - má-ču (ma chu) the first infusion of malt or stronger beer, v. čaṅ (chang). - ma-čúṅ (ma chung) Cs.: 'a mother's younger sister', perh. more correctly: a father's second wife, as to rank; ma-čén (ma chen) 1. Cs.: 'a mother's elder sister', or a father's principal wife. 2. v. the respective article. - ma-pár (ma par) capital and interest W. - ma-bu (ma bu) mother and son; capital and interest; original and copy; ma-bú mtúṅ-pa bri-ba (ma bu mthun pa 'bri ba) to copy accurately Schr.; a letter written above and below another letter; principal and cross beam etc. - má-mo (ma mo) v. that article. - ma-tsáb (ma tshab) foster-mother Sch. - ma-yźi (ma gzhi) v. sub II. - ma-ɣyár (ma g.yar) step-mother Cs. - ma-ró (ma ro) a mother's corpse Pth. II. a root signifying below, opp. to ya (ya): má-gi (ma gi) the lower one, e.g. čú-bo (chu bo) Mil.; ma-gi-na (ma gi na) below, at the bottom, má-gi-nas (ma gi nas) from below, out of the valley, in Sik.: from, out of, the Indian plain (v. mta (mtha')); má-mču (ma mchu) lower lip. - má-tem (ma them) sill, threshold. - má-tog (ma thog) v. tog (thog) I, 2. - má-rdo (ma rdo) = rmáṅ-rdo (rmang rdo). - ma-rábs (ma rabs) mean descent, people of low extraction Dzl. - ma-rí (ma ri) Sch. downward(?) - ma-ré (ma re) = ma-tém (ma them), v. re (re). III. negative adv. not, however only in some cases: a. in the simplest form of prohibition, where in the Tibetan language inst. of the imperative the root of the present with ma (ma) is used: ma ₒgro (ma 'gro) do not go, ma byed (ma byed) do not do (it). With the form of the future mi (mi) is placed: rjód-par mi byao (rjod par mi bya'o) it shall not, should not be pronounced Dom.; mi de dgrar mi bslaṅ (mi de dgrar mi bslang) they should not make the man their enemy S.g. - b. with the preterite: ma soṅ (ma song) he did not go, ma byas (ma byas) he did not do (it). - c. with the present tense also in conjunction with the words yin (yin), lags (lags), mčis (mchis), red (red). - d. without any evident reason, and perh. not always correctly, with many substantives and adjectives that are formed of infinitives or participles, and are conveying a negative sense: ma-ríg-pa (ma rig pa) a not knowing, ignorance; ma-rúṅ-ba (ma rung ba) v. ruṅ-ba (rung ba) (v. mi (mi)). IV. In the col. language of Lh. ma (ma) is used as an interrogative, when a question is returned by a question: *kyód-di miṅ či zer* (*khyod di ming ci zer*) what is your name? *miṅ ma?* (*ming ma?*) my name? V. Affix, so-called article, frq. denoting the fem. of the masc. in pa (pa), if mo (mo) is not used inst. of it; gen. put to the names of inanimate things, utensils etc., as also to compound adjectives: zaṅs ru-bźi-ma (zangs ru bzhi ma) a four-handled kettle (cf. bu lo-ɣnyís-pa (bu lo gnyis pa) a boy two years old, sub pa (pa)). VI. mai nyin (ma'i nyin) two days before yesterday C., = sṅón-ma žag (sngon ma zhag) W.
mā (mA) W. always with a marked accent and long vowel, prob. abbrev. of maṅs (mangs) very, before adjectives and adverbs, *mā máṅ-po* (*mA mang po*) very much, *mā gyál-la* (*mA rgyal la*) very good.
(Hind. مکار impostor) W. deceit, imposition, intrigue, *ma-kár čó-te zer* (*ma kar co te zer*) he speaks hypocritically, with some secret design; ma-kar-čan (ma kar can), hypocritical, fawning.
